units.title: Kinematics
units.overview:
Motion in one and two dimensions. Position, velocity, and acceleration as functions of time. Motion in two dimensions (projectile motion, circular motion).

Key equations:
• x(t) = x₀ + v₀t + ½at²
• v(t) = v₀ + at
• v² = v₀² + 2a(x - x₀)
• For projectiles: ax = 0, ay = -g
• Circular motion: a_c = v²/r

questions.text: A ball is launched from ground level at an angle θ = 35° above the horizontal with an initial speed v₀ = 20 m/s. Ignore air resistance.

parts.label: (a)
parts.text: Determine the horizontal and vertical components of the initial velocity.
parts.answer:
v₀ₓ = v₀ cos θ = 20 cos 35° = 16.4 m/s
v₀ᵧ = v₀ sin θ = 20 sin 35° = 11.5 m/s

parts.label: (b)
parts.text: Calculate the maximum height reached by the ball.
parts.answer:
At max height, vᵧ = 0
Using v² = v₀² + 2aΔy:
0 = (11.5)² + 2(-9.8)(Δy)
Δy = 132.25 / 19.6 = 6.75 m

parts.label: (c)
parts.text: Calculate the total time of flight.

parts.label: (d)
parts.text: Determine the horizontal range of the ball.
parts.answer:
R = v₀ₓ × t = 16.4 × 2.35 = 38.5 m

Alternatively: R = (v₀² sin 2θ)/g = (400 × sin 70°)/9.8 = 38.3 m

questions.text: A car travels along a straight road. Its position as a function of time is given by x(t) = 2t³ - 9t² + 12t, where x is in meters and t is in seconds.

parts.label: (a)
parts.text: Find expressions for the velocity v(t) and acceleration a(t).
parts.answer:
v(t) = dx/dt = 6t² - 18t + 12 m/s
a(t) = dv/dt = 12t - 18 m/s²

parts.label: (b)
parts.text: At what times is the car momentarily at rest?

parts.label: (c)
parts.text: Find the position of the car at each time it is at rest.

parts.label: (d)
parts.text: Determine the total distance traveled by the car from t = 0 to t = 3 s.

units.title: Newton's Laws of Motion
units.overview:
Newton's three laws. Free-body diagrams. Friction (static and kinetic). Circular motion and centripetal forces. Drag forces.

Key concepts:
• ΣF = ma (Newton's Second Law in component form)
• Static friction: f_s ≤ μ_s N
• Kinetic friction: f_k = μ_k N
• Centripetal force: F_c = mv²/r
• Always draw free-body diagrams first

questions.text: Two blocks are connected by a light string over a frictionless pulley (Atwood machine). Block A has mass m_A = 4 kg and block B has mass m_B = 6 kg.

parts.label: (a)
parts.text: Draw free-body diagrams for each block.
parts.answer:
Block A: Weight m_A·g downward, Tension T upward
Block B: Weight m_B·g downward, Tension T upward

Since m_B > m_A, block B accelerates downward and block A accelerates upward.

parts.label: (b)
parts.text: Derive an expression for the acceleration of the system in terms of m_A, m_B, and g.
parts.answer:
For block A (up is positive): T - m_A·g = m_A·a
For block B (down is positive): m_B·g - T = m_B·a

Adding both equations:
m_B·g - m_A·g = (m_A + m_B)·a

a = (m_B - m_A)·g / (m_A + m_B)

parts.label: (c)
parts.text: Calculate the numerical value of the acceleration.

parts.label: (d)
parts.text: Find the tension in the string.
parts.answer:
Using block A equation:
T = m_A(g + a) = 4(9.8 + 1.96) = 4(11.76) = 47.04 N

Or using the derived formula:
T = 2·m_A·m_B·g / (m_A + m_B) = 2(4)(6)(9.8)/10 = 47.04 N

units.title: Work, Energy, and Power
units.overview:
Work done by constant and variable forces. Kinetic energy and the work-energy theorem. Potential energy (gravitational and elastic). Conservation of energy. Power.

Key equations:
• W = F·d·cos θ (constant force)
• W = ∫F·dx (variable force)
• KE = ½mv²
• Work-Energy Theorem: W_net = ΔKE
• U_g = mgh, U_s = ½kx²
• P = dW/dt = F·v

questions.text: A 2 kg block slides down a frictionless ramp of height h = 3 m and then across a rough horizontal surface where μ_k = 0.4.

parts.label: (a)
parts.text: Find the speed of the block at the bottom of the ramp using energy conservation.
parts.answer:
Using conservation of energy (no friction on ramp):
mgh = ½mv²
v = √(2gh) = √(2 × 9.8 × 3) = √58.8 = 7.67 m/s

parts.label: (b)
parts.text: Determine the friction force on the block as it slides across the horizontal surface.
parts.answer:
On horizontal surface: N = mg = 2(9.8) = 19.6 N
f_k = μ_k · N = 0.4 × 19.6 = 7.84 N

parts.label: (c)
parts.text: How far does the block slide on the horizontal surface before stopping?
parts.answer:
Using work-energy theorem:
-f_k · d = 0 - ½mv²
d = mv² / (2f_k) = 2(58.8) / (2 × 7.84) = 7.5 m

Or equivalently: mgh = f_k · d → d = mgh/(μ_k mg) = h/μ_k = 3/0.4 = 7.5 m

parts.label: (d)
parts.text: Calculate the power dissipated by friction at the instant the block reaches the horizontal surface.
